texto: It is a well known fact that Abraham Lincoln spent much of his spare time visiting wounded soldiers in Union Army hospitals. I've spent thirty years teaching history at Columbia and I don't think I've spent more than fifteen minutes in the freshman dorm. Are we the ones keeping Lincoln's memory alive? Or are we burying it?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Eric Foner que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “It is a well known fact that Abraham Lincoln spent much of his spare time visiting wounded soldie…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, college, lincoln, com foco em lincoln.

Or are we burying it?

texto: Successful teaching rests both on a genuine and selfless concern for students and on the ability to convey to them a love of history.
explicacao: Citação de Eric Foner sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Successful teaching rests both on a genuine and selfless concern for students and on the ability …” articula educacao, education, reflexao em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

frase_original: Successful teaching rests both on a genuine and selfless concern for students and on the ability to convey to them a love of history.

texto: Look, Mrs. McGillicuddy, it's not my fault your son jumped out a dorm room window on Christmas eve. I've written over fifty books as a Columbia professor, all right? You don't do that by holding hands with every at-risk undergraduate who says he's homesick, or he's turning gay, or the dog ate his term paper. I write about Lincoln, and freedom, and great ideas. I don't always have time for students. It's like Dean Martin used to say: if you want to talk, go to a priest. Hey -- what's the gun for?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eric Foner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Look, Mrs. McGillicuddy, it's not my fault your son jumped out a dorm room window on Christmas ev…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, college, mcgillicuddy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de natal e humor.

frase_original: Look, Mrs. McGillicuddy, it's not my fault your son jumped out a dorm room window on Christmas eve. I've written over fifty books as a Columbia professor, all right? You don't do that by holding hands with every at-risk undergraduate who says he's homesick, or he's turning gay, or the dog ate his term paper. I write about Lincoln, and freedom, and great ideas. I don't always have time for students. It's like Dean Martin used to say: if you want to talk, go to a priest. Hey -- what's the gun for?
